target.title: fix(gateway): surface cross-agent subagent sessions via allAgents flag (#95295)
target.body: Fixes #95295. ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue where users viewing the Control UI Sessions page or Recent Sessions sidebar for one agent could not see child sessions that were spawned from that workflow but stored under another configured agent. In the reported `dev` -> `orchestrator` case, the cross-agent subagent sessions existed in the session stores and could be listed from the CLI, but the web UI hid them unless they belonged to the currently selected agent. ## Why This Change Was Made This PR makes the UI use the existing `configuredAgentsOnly: true` plus omitted `agentId` request shape when the `All agents` toggle is enabled, instead of adding a new `sessions.list` protocol field. Explicit per-agent refreshes still pass `agentId`, so scoped loads stay narrow while the all-agents view can surface cross-agent child sessions in both the Sessions table and Recent Sessions sidebar. ## User Impact With `All agents` off, behavior is unchanged: the selected agent shows only its own sessions. With `All agents` on, operators can now see cross-agent child sessions in the Sessions table and Recent Sessions sidebar, including subagent runs spawned from another configured agent that were previously invisible. ## Evidence - Live behavior proof used a real `pnpm openclaw --dev gateway` setup with two configured agents (`dev`, `orchestrator`), not seeded store files. - Three real `sessions_spawn` calls from `agent:dev:main` created `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` session records in the orchestrator store. The child runs later failed at model step in this dev environment because the OpenAI key returned `401`, but the session records were already persisted; this PR changes whether those persisted rows are visible in the UI. - Replace the three placeholders below with uploaded PR images after you attach them in GitHub: - `[IMAGE 1: Before fix - Recent Sessions sidebar only shows the current agent row]` - `[IMAGE 2: After fix - Recent Sessions sidebar shows current agent plus cross-agent orchestrator subagent rows with All agents enabled]` - `[IMAGE 3: Sessions page after fix - mixed dev and orchestrator rows visible in the same All agents view]` - Suggested captions once images are uploaded: - Before fix: cross-agent child sessions already exist in store, but the sidebar still shows only the current agent. - After fix: enabling `All agents` surfaces the cross-agent `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` rows in Recent Sessions. - Sessions page after fix: the table now shows mixed `dev` and `orchestrator` rows in the same view. - Focused validation: - `pnpm test src/gateway/server.sessions.store-rpc.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.sidebar.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.subagent.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils` - `pnpm tsgo:core` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - `pnpm oxfmt --check` on touched files - `pnpm protocol:gen:swift` - `git diff --check`

target.title: fix: avoid stale dashboard child context budgets
target.body:
Closes #97331

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users opening a new Control UI dashboard chat could see and use a stale inherited context budget when the parent dashboard session had cached context metadata from an older model/config state.

## Why This Change Was Made

Dashboard child sessions still inherit the parent runtime selection that represents user intent, including provider/model/auth profile and run settings. They no longer inherit cached context or usage metadata, so the new session can derive the current budget from the active model/config/catalog.

## User Impact

New Control UI dashboard chats no longer get pinned to a stale smaller context window from their parent session. For the observed `github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8` case, a child session can derive the current 1M budget instead of carrying forward the parent's cached 128k budget.

## Evidence

Before evidence:

```shell
# Active model/config evidence, redacted to field names and values:
"primary": "github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8"
"contextTokens": 1000000
"id": "claude-opus-4.8"
"contextWindow": 1000000
"contextTokens": 1000000

# Fresh child transcript before this fix showed the stale inherited budget:
{"type":"model_change","provider":"github-copilot","modelId":"claude-opus-4.8"}
📚 Context: 16k/128k (13%)
"parentSessionKey":"[redacted dashboard parent session key]","sessionId":"[redacted session id]","model":"claude-opus-4.8","contextTokens":128000,"status":"running"
```

After-fix local proof:

```shell
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
 ✓ |gateway-server| ../../src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts > sessions.create inherits parent runtime model selection without stale context metadata 34ms
 ✓ |gateway-methods| ../../src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts > sessions.create inherits parent runtime model selection without stale context metadata 26ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  26 passed (26)
   Duration  84.99s (transform 74.69s, setup 2.11s, import 18.97s, tests 63.20s, environment 1ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 105.95s

$ git diff --check
# no output

$ .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.86)
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Newly created dashboard child sessions no longer inherit stale parent `contextTokens`, `inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `totalTokens`, `totalTokensFresh`, or `contextBudgetStatus` metadata while still inheriting runtime model selection.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout worktree on `upstream/main` at `36722014ef`, branch `bug-050-dashboard-contexttokens`, Node `v26.3.0`, pnpm `11.2.2` after `corepack pnpm install`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.

Evidence after fix:

```shell
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
 ✓ |gateway-server| ../../src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts > sessions.create inherits parent runtime model selection without stale context metadata 34ms
 ✓ |gateway-methods| ../../src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts > sessions.create inherits parent runtime model selection without stale context metadata 26ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  26 passed (26)
   Duration  84.99s (transform 74.69s, setup 2.11s, import 18.97s, tests 63.20s, environment 1ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 105.95s
```

Observed result after fix: The regression test creates a parent entry with stale context and usage metadata, then verifies the child session keeps the intended runtime provider/model/auth settings while all stale context/usage metadata fields are absent so current model metadata can be resolved normally.

What was not tested: I did not rerun a live Control UI browser session against the private affected dashboard chain after the code patch; the reproducible seam is covered at `sessions.create`, which is where the stale metadata was copied.

target.title: fix(googlechat): fall back to text link when remote media upload fails with 403
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #89430 reports that Google Chat agents using app-auth (service-account, `chat.bot` scope) cannot deliver replies that include image or file attachments. The official Google Chat API contract requires user auth for `media.upload`; app-auth receives a 403. Three delivery paths attempted the upload without a fallback and silently discarded the reply.
- **Root Cause**: All three paths call `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` with no error handling for the known scope limitation. `withGoogleChatResponse` throws `"Google Chat upload 403: …"` for HTTP 403 on the upload endpoint (controlled by `errorPrefix: "Google Chat upload"` at `api.ts:228`), providing a precise prefix that distinguishes upload auth failures from `sendGoogleChatMessage` 403s (which use the default `"Google Chat API"` prefix). The three affected paths are: (1) outbound adapter `sendMedia` (`channel.adapters.ts:313`), (2) monitor reply delivery `sendMedia` (`monitor-reply-delivery.ts:114`), (3) message action `send`/`upload-file` with media (`actions.ts:143`).
- **Fix**: Export `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` from `api.ts` (matches `"Google Chat upload 403:"` exactly to avoid false positives) and apply a narrow try/catch around only `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` in all three paths. On 403 for a remote http(s) URL, fall back to `sendGoogleChatMessage` with the URL as text joined with caption/message. Preserve the original error contract: `readRemoteMediaBuffer` and `sendGoogleChatMessage` each keep their own catch blocks (monitor/adapter paths stay always-resolving); the actions path re-throws non-403 upload failures.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts` — export `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` (pure string check, no runtime deps).
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.adapters.ts` — import helper; narrow try/catch to upload only; 403 + remote URL → text link fallback.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor-reply-delivery.ts` — import helper; split `sendMedia` into per-phase catch blocks; 403 + remote URL → text link fallback.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.ts` — import helper; narrow try/catch to upload only; 403 + remote URL → text link fallback; non-403 re-throws.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts` — 2 new tests: adapter 403 fallback; adapter non-403 re-throws.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` — 5 new tests: 403 with caption; 403 URL-only; non-403 logs error; buffer-load failure logs error; post-upload send failure logs error.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts` — 2 new tests: action 403 fallback; action non-403 re-throws.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Upload behavior for non-403 errors is unchanged; actions path re-throws, adapter/monitor paths log.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin SDK surface unchanged. Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure Google Chat with service-account app auth (`chat.bot` scope).
2. Send a message (via agent reply or message tool) that includes a remote image or file URL.
3. **Before this PR**: `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` receives a 403 in all three paths; reply is dropped.
4. **After this PR**: 403 detected; all three paths fall back to `sendGoogleChatMessage` with `[caption, mediaUrl].join("\n")` — reply reaches the user as a text link.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#89430)**: when `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` throws `"Google Chat upload 403: …"` for a remote http(s) URL, all three delivery paths send a text-link fallback.

**Real environment tested (Ubuntu Server Node 24, source-level — Vitest against production `deliverGoogleChatReply`, `googlechatOutboundAdapter.attachedResults.sendMedia`, and `googlechatMessageActions.handleAction` with mocked `uploadGoogleChatAttachment`)**: fallback tests call production functions with the mocked upload throwing the exact error format from `withGoogleChatResponse`. No live credentials available; API contract source-confirmed via `api.ts:59` and `auth.ts:11`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` — 35 tests pass (26 pre-existing + 9 new). `pnpm exec oxlint` clean. `pnpm format:check` clean.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
Tests  35 passed (35)
```

**What was not tested**: live Google Chat end-to-end with a real service-account credential.

**Repro confirmation**: 403 fallback tests fail on the pre-patch tree (upload error propagates or is dropped, `sendGoogleChatMessage` not called with fallback text) and pass with the fix applied.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` fires on non-upload 403s. **Mitigation**: matches `"Google Chat upload 403:"` (set by `errorPrefix: "Google Chat upload"` at `api.ts:228`); `sendGoogleChatMessage` uses `"Google Chat API"` prefix so its 403s never match.
- **Risk**: Behavioral regression in monitor path — buffer load or send failures swallowed. **Mitigation**: each phase has its own catch block restoring the original always-resolving contract; regression tests cover both.
- **Risk**: Actions path behavior change — non-403 upload errors now re-throw instead of propagating silently. **Mitigation**: the original path also propagated upload errors (no catch existed); re-throw is equivalent behavior. Test confirms non-403 re-throws.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Google Chat plugin

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #89430

target.title: fix(cli): replace hardcoded "--" with FLAG_TERMINATOR constant in getCommandPathInternal
target.body:
## Summary

Replace the sole remaining hardcoded `"--"` literal in `getCommandPathInternal` with the `FLAG_TERMINATOR` constant already imported and used by every other function in `argv.ts`.

Prevents silent subcommand-routing divergence if `FLAG_TERMINATOR` is ever changed.

**Addressed review feedback (ClawSweeper P3):** Reworded the regression test comment to accurately describe what it proves (integration with the constant), since the test cannot independently detect a literal regression while `FLAG_TERMINATOR` remains `"--"`. The companion contract test asserts the canonical value.

## Linked context

Closes #83902

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** `getCommandPathInternal` used a hardcoded `"--"` string while all other functions in the same file use the `FLAG_TERMINATOR` constant.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node v22.22.3, latest upstream/main (050c0813b)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
npx tsx proof-89897.mjs
```

- **Evidence after fix (copied live output):**

```
=== Live Proof: #89897 / #83902 ===
FLAG_TERMINATOR constant: "--"

Case 1: getCommandPath([node, openclaw, channels, --, add], 2)
  Result: [channels]
  PASS: true

Case 2: getCommandPath([node, openclaw, channels, add], 2)
  Result: [channels, add]
  PASS: true

Case 3: getCommandPath([node, openclaw, config, --, set, --key, value], 2)
  Result: [config]
  PASS: true

Case 4: getCommandPathWithRootOptions([node, openclaw, --debug, status, --, extra], 2)
  Result: [status]
  PASS: true

Case 5: FLAG_TERMINATOR === "--"
  PASS: true
```

target.title: fix(google): rotate Gemini API keys for LLM requests
target.body:
Related: #97314

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with multiple Gemini API keys configured would still have Google LLM streaming requests fail immediately when the first key hit a rate limit or quota response. The embedding path already used API-key rotation, but the LLM stream path resolved only one key before sending the provider request.

## Why This Change Was Made

This routes official Google Generative AI stream setup through the existing provider API-key rotation helper before any SSE chunks are emitted. The fix keeps credential boundaries explicit: env-key rotation is allowed only for the HTTPS official Google Generative AI host, while Vertex, OAuth JSON credential requests, custom/proxy Gemini base URLs, and non-TLS Gemini base URLs stay out of global Gemini env-key rotation.

## User Impact

Users can configure multiple Gemini keys and recover from first-key 429/quota responses for official HTTPS Gemini LLM streaming requests instead of manually switching keys or seeing a hard failure. OAuth JSON credential requests continue to use bearer auth without falling back to configured API keys, and custom/proxy or non-TLS Gemini endpoints receive only the caller-provided endpoint credential.

## Evidence

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm format -- extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/api-key-rotation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts`
  - Passed: agents config 2 files / 42 tests; extension-providers config 1 file / 72 tests.
- `git diff --check`
- Redacted runtime proof using `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with the real Google transport stream and guarded-fetch path plus a local fetch stub. The proof keys are fake labels and no secret value was printed:

```text
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro method=POST url=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.5-pro:streamGenerateContent timeoutMs=undefined proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro status=429 elapsedMs=33 contentType=application/json
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro method=POST url=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.5-pro:streamGenerateContent timeoutMs=undefined proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro status=200 elapsedMs=3 contentType=text/event-stream
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=custom-google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro method=POST url=https://proxy.example.com/gemini/v1beta/models/gemini-2.5-pro:streamGenerateContent timeoutMs=undefined proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=custom-google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro status=429 elapsedMs=1 contentType=application/json
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro method=POST url=http://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.5-pro:streamGenerateContent timeoutMs=undefined proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=google api=google-generative-ai model=gemini-2.5-pro status=429 elapsedMs=1 contentType=application/json
Google Gemini key rotation runtime proof (redacted)
official stopReason=stop
official text=recovered
official request 1: protocol=https: host=generativelanguage.googleapis.com key=proof-key-1(redacted) status=429
official request 2: protocol=https: host=generativelanguage.googleapis.com key=proof-key-2(redacted) status=200
custom stopReason=error
custom request 1: protocol=https: host=proxy.example.com key=explicit-proxy-key(redacted) status=429
custom totalRequests=1
http stopReason=error
http request 1: protocol=http: host=generativelanguage.googleapis.com key=explicit-http-key(redacted) status=429
http totalRequests=1
```

- ClawSweeper findings addressed:
  - Custom/proxy endpoint key sharing fixed in `8f3654c2f3b`: key collection receives `model.baseUrl` and refuses global Gemini env-key rotation unless the endpoint is the official Google host; regression coverage asserts a custom Gemini proxy gets one request with only `explicit-proxy-key`.
  - Non-TLS official-host key sharing fixed in `ef1ec1c3e0f`: the official gate now requires `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com`, and regression coverage asserts `http://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta` gets one request with only `explicit-http-key`.

AI-assisted: yes. No agent transcript is included because explicit transcript approval was not given.

target.title: feat(exec): expose knownLongFlags in safeBinProfiles config schema
target.body:
## Summary

Expose the `knownLongFlags` field in the `safeBinProfiles` config schema, allowing operators to declare long flags as "known" for GNU abbreviation resolution without misusing `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`.

Closes #56805

> **Rebased 2026-06-10.** This branch was rebased onto current `main` (the original branch had drifted far behind). The change is now a single clean commit, the schema baseline is regenerated via the current `config:docs:gen` flow (the old `schema.base.generated.ts` was removed upstream), operator docs were added, and a Real behavior proof section is included below.

## Problem

The `knownLongFlags` field exists internally in `SafeBinProfile` and is used by `validateSafeBinArgv` for GNU long-option abbreviation resolution. However, it is not present in:

1. The Zod schema (`ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema`) — `.strict()` rejects it as an unknown key
2. The `SafeBinProfileFixture` type — no way to pass it through config normalization

For empty profiles `{}`, `knownLongFlags` auto-derives as `[]`, causing **all** long flags to be treated as unknown and rejected. The only workaround is to list every possible long flag in `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`, which changes their semantics.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags: z.array(z.string()).optional()` to `ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema` |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy-profiles.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to `SafeBinProfileFixture`; normalize it in `normalizeSafeBinProfileFixture`; merge user-provided with auto-derived flags in `compileSafeBinProfile`, filtering strict prefixes of denied flags |
| `src/config/normalize-exec-safe-bin.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to the type cast in normalization |
| `docs/tools/exec-approvals-advanced.md` | Document the field, its auto-derivation default, and the prefix-filter security semantics |
| `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` | Regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen` for the new field |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` | 8 new tests for the config path + security filter |

## Design Decisions

- **Merge, don't replace**: User-provided `knownLongFlags` are merged with auto-derived flags from `allowedValueFlags ∪ deniedFlags`, so flags in those lists stay "known" even if not explicitly listed.
- **Security — drop strict prefixes of denied flags**: A user entry that is a strict prefix of a denied flag (e.g. `--rec` when `--recursive` is denied) is filtered at compile time, so it cannot gain exact-match priority in `resolveCanonicalLongFlag` and bypass the denial. (Addresses the prior Codex review P1.)
- **Reuses `normalizeFixtureFlags`**: Same normalization (trim, dedupe, sort) as other flag arrays.
- **Backwards-compatible**: Existing configs without `knownLongFlags` work identically via auto-derivation.

## Usage

```json5
{
  tools: {
    exec: {
      safeBins: ["mycli"],
      safeBinProfiles: {
        mycli: {
          allowedValueFlags: ["--limit"],
          deniedFlags: ["--recursive"],
          knownLongFlags: ["--dry-run", "--verbose"],
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Setting `tools.exec.safeBinProfiles.<bin>.knownLongFlags` in a real config previously could not be accepted — the strict Zod schema had no such key. After this patch the field is accepted by config validation and is honored by the safe-bin argv validator (declared long flags resolve, including GNU abbreviation), while strict prefixes of denied flags are still blocked.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw build from this branch, `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (bb6e477)`, Node v22.22.0, run via the real `openclaw` CLI and the compiled `exec-safe-bin-policy` module (not mocks).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
# 1) Real CLI accepts a config containing knownLongFlags
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/klf-proof.json5 openclaw config validate --json
# config: { tools: { exec: { safeBins: ["mycli"], safeBinProfiles: {
#   mycli: { allowedValueFlags: ["--limit"], deniedFlags: ["--recursive"],
#            knownLongFlags: ["--dry-run", "--verbose"] } } } } }

# 2) Same CLI still rejects an unknown key (the pre-fix state of knownLongFlags)
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/klf-proof-unknown.json5 openclaw config validate --json
#   ( mycli: { knownLongFlagsTYPO: ["--dry-run"] } )

# 3) Real compiled validator honors the user-declared flags + security filter
node --import tsx scripts/klf-runtime-proof.mts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
# step 1 — openclaw config validate (knownLongFlags present)
{"valid":true,"path":"/tmp/klf-proof.json5"}

# step 2 — openclaw config validate (unknown key knownLongFlagsTYPO)
{"valid":false,"path":"/tmp/klf-proof-unknown.json5",
 "issues":[{"path":"tools.exec.safeBinProfiles.mycli","message":"Invalid input"}]}

# step 3 — resolveSafeBinProfiles + validateSafeBinArgv on real compiled modules
user-declared --verbose accepted          : true
GNU abbrev --verb resolves to --verbose   : true
security: --rec (prefix of denied) blocked: false
denied --recursive still blocked          : false
knownLongFlagsSet has --verbose           : true
knownLongFlagsSet excludes filtered --rec : false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The live `openclaw config validate` returns `valid: true` for a config carrying `knownLongFlags`, and still returns `valid: false` for an unknown sibling key — confirming the field is genuinely wired into the strict schema. At runtime the compiled validator accepts the user-declared `--verbose`, resolves the `--verb` abbreviation to it, keeps `--recursive` denied, and drops the `--rec` prefix so it cannot bypass that denial.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end exec of a real third-party binary through the approval pipeline (requires a trusted-dir executable); behavior is covered at the policy/validator layer instead.

## Test Plan

- [x] All tests in `exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` pass: **98 passed** (90 existing + 8 new) under `vitest.infra.config.ts`
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:check` and `pnpm config:schema:check` pass (baseline in sync, no drift)
- [x] `oxlint` clean on changed files; no new type errors introduced
- [x] Real behavior proof captured above via the `openclaw` CLI and compiled validator

The PR marks manual cron runs, prevents manual outcomes from consuming `deleteAfterRun` or scheduled retry state, adds `lastRunWasManual` to cron state/protocol schemas, and updates cron tests.

PR surface: Source +65, Tests +852. Total +917 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main manual-run completion calls `applyJobResult` without a manual discriminator, and successful `deleteAfterRun` at-jobs reach the scheduled-run deletion branch. I did not run a live current-main reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (includes all 12 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **55 tests passed** (284ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (55 tests) 284ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **10 tests passed** (63ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 63ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 13ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1000 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1000 passed (1002)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 new regression tests pass, confirming that manual runs preserve one-shot jobs (`deleteAfterRun`), do not increment/reset counters, do not disable at-jobs on error, do not rewrite recurring `nextRunAtMs` with backoff, and do not clear failure-alert cooldown. Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **11 tests passed** (92ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (11 tests) 92ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 10ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job → NOT deleted ✅
  - Manual error → counters NOT incremented ✅
  - Manual success → counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared ✅
  - Manual at-job error → NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual every-job error → backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped at-job → consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` → failure alert NOT emitted ✅

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`, 2026-06-02)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `lastRunWasManual` field was missing from the `CronJobStateSchema` and `CronJobStatePatchSchema` in `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/cron.ts`. Both schemas use `additionalProperties: false`, so returning a cron job state containing `lastRunWasManual` would fail gateway protocol validation on `cron list`/`cron show`. Added `lastRunWasManual: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean())` to both schemas.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`), Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `ccb89e3f11`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On the previous commit (`45efd1abdd`), `CronJobStateSchema` did not include `lastRunWasManual`. Any cron job state object with `lastRunWasManual: true` (set by `timer.ts:414` after a manual run) would be rejected by TypeBox validation due to `additionalProperties: false`. The `CronJobStatePatchSchema` was similarly missing the field, preventing state patches that include it.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" \
  node:24-bookworm \
  bash -c '
    cd /app && npm install 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
      src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts 2>&1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 335ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 54ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 31ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts (3 tests) 3ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts (2 tests) 0ms

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  86 passed (86)
   Start at  07:37:28
   Duration  3.44s (transform 2.31s, setup 280ms, import 2.62s, tests 424ms, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 86 tests pass including:
  - `timer.regression.test.ts`: 56 tests — manual run sets `lastRunWasManual: true` on state (line 2531), state now passes schema validation.
  - `service.restart-catchup.test.ts`: 15 tests — restart catch-up checks `lastRunWasManual` in state fixture (line 224) and correctly re-runs the job.
  - `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`: 3 tests — schema conformance verified (delivery modes, status shape, failover reasons).
  - `cron-protocol-schema.test.ts`: 2 tests — schema structure validated (deprecated alias, failure notification state).

- **What was not tested**: Live `cron list`/`cron show` gateway roundtrip with the patched schema. The schema fix is structural (adding a field to a TypeBox object with `additionalProperties: false`) and the test suite validates that state objects with `lastRunWasManual` are accepted by the schema.

## Live Gateway CLI roundtrip proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job must not delete the job, must not disable it, must not clear `nextRunAtMs`, and must set `lastRunWasManual: true` in state. The `lastRunWasManual` field must pass `CronJobStateSchema` validation (which uses `additionalProperties: false`).
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64, OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, OpenClaw Gateway built from source at commit `ccb89e3f11`.
- **Steps**:
  1. Built OpenClaw from source on the PR branch
  2. Started Gateway inside Docker with `gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth token`
  3. Created a `deleteAfterRun` at-job via `cron add`
  4. Manually ran the job via `cron run` (resulted in error due to missing codex-linux-arm64 in Docker — this is the interesting case: manual error on a deleteAfterRun job)
  5. Verified job survival, `lastRunWasManual: true`, `enabled: true`, and `nextRunAtMs` preserved via `cron show` and `cron list`
  6. Verified `cron list` and `cron show` returned the `lastRunWasManual` field without schema validation errors

<details>
<summary>Terminal output (full Docker CLI roundtrip)</summary>

```

OS: Linux 6.19.13-orbstack-gbd1dc07b8cf4 aarch64

  "enabled": true,
  "deleteAfterRun": true,

  "schedule": {
    "kind": "at",
    "at": "2026-12-31T15:59:59.000Z"
  },

  "state": {
    "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000
  }
}

=== Step 2: cron list (before manual run) ===

  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
      "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
      "enabled": true,
      "deleteAfterRun": true,
      "state": { "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000 },
      "status": "idle"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1
}

=== Step 3: cron show (before manual run) ===
{
  "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
  "enabled": true,
  "deleteAfterRun": true,
  "state": { "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000 },
  "status": "idle"
}

=== Step 4: cron run (manual execution) ===
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01:1780389607244:1"
}

=== Step 5: cron show (AFTER manual run) ===
{
  "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
  "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
  "enabled": true,                          ← NOT disabled by manual error
  "deleteAfterRun": true,                   ← still set, not consumed
  "state": {
    "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000,           ← preserved, not cleared
    "lastRunAtMs": 1780389607245,
    "lastRunStatus": "error",
    "lastRunWasManual": true,               ← NEW FIELD: schema accepted it
    "lastStatus": "error",
    "lastDurationMs": 15835
  },
  "status": "error"
}

=== Step 6: cron list (AFTER manual run - verify job still exists) ===
{
  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
      "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
      "enabled": true,
      "deleteAfterRun": true,
      "state": {
        "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000,
        "lastRunStatus": "error",
        "lastRunWasManual": true,
        "lastStatus": "error"
      },
      "status": "error"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1
}

=== Step 7: cron runs (run history) ===
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "jobId": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
      "action": "finished",

      "runId": "manual:d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01:1780389607244:1",
      "runAtMs": 1780389607245,

      "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000,
      "jobName": "proof-delete-after-run"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1
}

Gateway stopped.
=== PROOF COMPLETE ===
```

</details>

- **Observed result**: After manual `cron run` (which triggered an error due to missing codex dependency in Docker):
  - Job **survived** — still present in `cron list` and `cron show` ✅
  - `enabled: true` — manual error did NOT disable the at-job ✅
  - `nextRunAtMs: 1798732799000` — preserved, NOT cleared ✅
  - `deleteAfterRun: true` — still set, NOT consumed by manual run ✅
  - `lastRunWasManual: true` — new field present and accepted by schema ✅
  - No `consecutiveErrors` field — manual error did NOT increment counter ✅
  - Run history shows `runId: "manual:..."` prefix confirming manual execution path ✅
  - Both `cron show` and `cron list` returned `lastRunWasManual` without schema validation errors, proving `CronJobStateSchema` correctly includes the field ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` (would require a working LLM provider in Docker). The `shouldDelete` guard path (`wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual`) is exercised by unit tests. Multi-node cluster, production load.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 — backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` — blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Unit tests in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c \
  "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts"

# Live Gateway CLI in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  -e OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-test \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=test-token-123 \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c '
    mkdir -p /tmp/openclaw-test
    echo "{\"gateway\":{\"port\":19001,\"bind\":\"loopback\"}}" > /tmp/openclaw-test/openclaw.json
    node openclaw.mjs gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --token test-token-123 --port 19001 &
    sleep 8
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-at-delete --at "2026-06-02T23:00:00Z" --message reminder --delete-after-run --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>, cron list
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-recurring --every 10s --message "recurring check" --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>
    kill %1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

<details>
<summary>Docker unit test output (82 tests pass)</summary>

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (57 tests) 254ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 44ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 25ms

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  82 passed (82)
   Start at  11:08:37
   Duration  2.77s (transform 1.83s, setup 250ms, import 2.09s, tests 323ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.85s
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>Docker Gateway CLI output</summary>

```
=== Node.js version ===
v24.16.0
=== Starting Gateway ===
[gateway] ready

TEST 1: Create deleteAfterRun at-job
  → id: dbfbe16b-c44a-4aa1-b820-889dbdbcb9a2, deleteAfterRun: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780441200000

TEST 2: Manual run on deleteAfterRun at-job
  → { "ok": true, "enqueued": true, "runId": "manual:dbfbe16b-..." }

TEST 3: Verify at-job survived manual run
  → id: dbfbe16b-..., enabled: true, deleteAfterRun: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780441200000 (PRESERVED)
  → status: "running" (agent executing but will error — no LLM provider in Docker)

TEST 4: Create recurring every-10s job
  → id: 18b2583a-7990-4667-95cd-591614c858f9, everyMs: 10000, nextRunAtMs: 1780398505454

TEST 5: Manual run on recurring job
  → { "ok": true, "enqueued": true, "runId": "manual:18b2583a-..." }

TEST 6: Verify recurring job state after manual run
  → id: 18b2583a-..., enabled: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780398505454 (PRESERVED, NOT pushed by backoff)

TEST 7: Schema validation — cron list --json
  → Both jobs returned with valid schema, no validation errors
  → total: 2, both enabled, both with preserved nextRunAtMs

Gateway stopped.
```

</details>

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. **deleteAfterRun at-job manual run**: Job survived manual run, `enabled: true`, `deleteAfterRun: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅
  2. **Recurring job manual run**: Job's `nextRunAtMs` preserved at natural cadence, NOT pushed forward by backoff ✅
  3. **Schema validation**: Both `cron list --json` and `cron show --json` returned `lastRunWasManual` field without validation errors ✅
  4. **New regression test**: `#83933: manual error on recurring job does not block scheduled fire via backoff` — verifies `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` returns `undefined` for manual errors, `isRunnableJob()` returns `true` within theoretical backoff window, `recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance()` computes natural cadence (not backoff floor), and control case confirms scheduled errors still apply backoff ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` on recurring job (requires working LLM in Docker). Multi-node cluster, production load, actual delivery to chat channels.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`, and unify the cron source-delivery planner into a single shared helper used by both the normal execution path and the version-skew fallback.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. **Unified planner**: Extracted `resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` as the single canonical cron source-delivery planner in `source-delivery-fallback.ts`. Both the normal cron execution path (`run.ts`) and the version-skew fallback path (`run-executor.ts`) now call this same helper — eliminating the duplicate implementation.
2. The helper calls `resolveCronDeliveryPlan(job)` to determine the delivery mode from the job config, then delegates to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` with parameters matching **current main's semantics**:
   - **webhook**: `owner: "none"`, message tool **disabled**, no direct fallback
   - **none**: `owner: "none"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, no direct fallback
   - **announce**: `owner: "direct_fallback"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, `directDelivery: true`, `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from resolved delivery status
3. `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` is a thin wrapper that derives the `CronDeliveryPlan` from the job and calls the shared helper
4. **Removed all legacy field sniffing** — no `toolPolicy`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, or `messageChannel` reads from stale callers. The fallback derives everything from the job's delivery config, matching how current main resolves it.
5. Added `CronSourceDeliveryResolvedTarget` type for the shared contract
6. Added `logWarn` diagnostic when fallback activates

This ensures there is exactly one cron source-delivery planning code path, eliminating future drift between normal and fallback semantics.

## Tests

20 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` unit tests (4 tests):**
1. `delivery.mode "none"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_none"`, message tool enabled but not forced, no direct fallback
2. `delivery.mode "announce"` → `owner: "direct_fallback"`, `reason: "cron_announce"`, direct fallback enabled, `skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from `resolvedDelivery.ok`
3. `delivery.mode "webhook"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_webhook"`, message tool **disabled**, empty target
4. Isolated agentTurn with no delivery config → defaults to announce behavior

**`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` parity tests (4 tests):**
5. `none` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
6. `announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
7. `webhook` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
8. `implicit announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (4 tests):**
9. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "none"` → falls back with `messageToolForced: false`, `owner: "none"`
10. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "announce"` → `direct_fallback` semantics, `forceMessageTool: false`
11. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "webhook"` → message tool disabled
12. No delivery config on isolated agentTurn → announce behavior fallback

**`executeCronRun` level (4 tests):**
13. Stale legacy params (`sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy.forceMessageTool`) → **ignored**, uses job delivery config instead
14. Valid `sourceDelivery` → pass-through, no fallback (regression)
15. Webhook mode → `disableMessageTool: true`, `forceMessageTool: false`
16. Announce mode → `direct_fallback` defaults, `disableMessageTool: false`, `forceMessageTool: false`

## Real behavior proof (current head)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Issue #85051 — isolated cron executor crashed with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')` when a version-skew/stale caller invoked the executor without a `sourceDelivery` argument. This branch guards that path via the shared planner `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` (reconstructing a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` from job delivery config + `resolvedDelivery`), and preserves duplicate-announce suppression: in announce mode, when `resolvedDelivery.ok` is missing/undefined it defaults to `true` so `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` stays on (no double-post).

- **Real environment tested**: Checkout commit `8c684df354ac9cff91e0197d29787e3ed9534b31` (branch `fix/cron-isolated-source-delivery-guard`). Primary clean-room run inside Docker `node:24-bookworm` (Node `v24.16.0`, Linux `aarch64`, fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` inside the container with host `node_modules` shadowed by an anonymous volume so Linux-native binaries are used). Cross-checked on host: macOS 15 (Darwin 24.3.0) arm64, Node `v24.10.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`. Harness runs `tsx` against the LIVE `src/` source (no mocks of the planner).

- **Failing proof before fix**: The pre-guard upstream/main shape is the raw dereference at `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts:182` on `upstream/main`:

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | sed -n '182p'
    const sourceReplyDeliveryMode = params.sourceDelivery.sourceReplyDeliveryMode;
  ```

  `upstream/main` has no `source-delivery-fallback.ts` (the guard does not exist there):

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts
  fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts' exists on disk, but not in 'upstream/main'
  ```

  The harness reproduces that exact dereference against a stale-caller params object that omits `sourceDelivery`. It throws the issue's exact error (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   A. STALE-CALLER CRASH (pre-guard upstream/main dereference)
  ============================================================
  thrown: true
  error.name: TypeError
  error.message: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
  PASS: pre-guard deref throws TypeError on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: error message matches "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')"
  ```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  ```
  # Docker (primary, clean room)
  docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/repo -v /repo/node_modules -w /repo \
    -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node:24-bookworm bash -lc '
      corepack enable
      corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts'

  # Host (cross-check)
  node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Guarded reconstruction for `announce` / `none` / `webhook` with `sourceDelivery` OMITTED — no crash, valid plans rebuilt (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   B. GUARDED reconstruction (this branch, sourceDelivery OMITTED)
  ============================================================
  announce plan: {
    "owner": "direct_fallback",
  ```

  Duplicate-suppression behavior across `resolvedDelivery.ok` variants, plus normal-path/fallback parity:

  ```
  ============================================================
   C. DUP-SUPPRESSION (announce; resolvedDelivery.ok variants)
  ============================================================
  ok=MISSING -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok MISSING -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true (suppression preserved)
  ok=false   -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: false
  PASS: ok=false -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === false
  ok=true    -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok=true -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true
  PASS: normal-path planner matches fallback wrapper when ok missing

  ============================================================
   RESULT: 22 passed, 0 failed
  ============================================================
  ```

  Existing regression suites (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  === vitest: source-delivery-guard ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts (17 tests) 15ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  17 passed (17)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.41s

  === vitest: double-announce ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts (68 tests) 218ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  68 passed (68)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.96s
  ```

  Host cross-check (macOS arm64, Node v24.10.0) — same results: harness `22 passed, 0 failed`; vitest `17 passed (17)` and `68 passed (68)`.

- **Observed result after fix**: With `sourceDelivery` omitted (the version-skew stale-caller path), the pre-guard upstream deref throws the exact `TypeError` from issue #85051, while this branch's `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` reconstructs a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` for every delivery mode (`announce` → `direct_fallback`/`cron_announce`, `none` → `none`/`cron_none`, `webhook` → `none`/`cron_webhook`) without crashing. Duplicate-announce suppression is preserved precisely as designed: missing `resolvedDelivery.ok` → `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true` (suppression on, so a message-tool send already delivered to the target does NOT trigger a second direct-fallback post); `ok:false` → `false`; `ok:true` → `true`. The normal-path planner (`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run.ts`) and the fallback wrapper (`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run-executor.ts`) produce identical plans for the missing-`ok` case, confirming both seams share one policy.

- **What was not tested**: No live Telegram/Discord/transport round-trip was exercised; there is no real message bus in this harness. The proof exercises the planner + executor source-delivery boundary directly at the LIVE source level (the function whose contract the guard protects), plus the colocated Vitest suites that cover the executor wiring (`createCronPromptExecutor` / `executeCronRun`) and the double-announce timer fallback. End-to-end delivery dispatch through an actual channel adapter, and the actual cross-version skew between a stale compiled caller and the new executor in a deployed gateway, were not reproduced.


Fixes #85051
