target.title: fix: prevent empty Anthropic error tool results
target.body:
Closes #97292

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running GitHub Copilot Claude models would see agent runs fail before the assistant could reply when replay included an Anthropic error `tool_result` whose content became empty.

## Why This Change Was Made

The legacy Anthropic provider path now treats empty text-only tool results as invalid at the provider boundary. Empty error results are replaced with `[tool error with no output]`, empty non-error results keep the existing `(no output)` placeholder, and empty text blocks are removed from image tool results before the outbound payload is sent.

## User Impact

Affected GitHub Copilot Claude runs can continue past empty error tool-result replay instead of surfacing a provider schema failure. Meaningful non-empty tool output is preserved.

## Evidence

Before evidence, from redacted retained Gateway logs:

```shell
{"event":"embedded_run_agent_end","runId":"[redacted run id]","isError":true,"error":"LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload.","failoverReason":"format","model":"claude-opus-4.7","provider":"github-copilot","rawErrorPreview":"400 {\"type\":\"error\",\"error\":{\"type\":\"invalid_request_error\",\"message\":\"messages.12.content.0.tool_result: content cannot be empty if `is_error` is true\"},\"request_id\":\"sha256:572439d01edf\"}","httpCode":"400","providerRuntimeFailureKind":"schema","providerErrorType":"invalid_request_error","providerErrorMessagePreview":"messages.12.content.0.tool_result: content cannot be empty if `is_error` is true","requestIdHash":"sha256:572439d01edf"} time="2026-06-26T17:35:22.414+00:00" traceId="[redacted trace id]"
{"event":"embedded_run_failover_decision","runId":"[redacted run id]","stage":"assistant","decision":"surface_error","failoverReason":"format","provider":"github-copilot","model":"claude-opus-4.7","fallbackConfigured":false,"timedOut":false,"aborted":false,"httpCode":"400","providerRuntimeFailureKind":"schema","providerErrorType":"invalid_request_error","providerErrorMessagePreview":"messages.12.content.0.tool_result: content cannot be empty if `is_error` is true","requestIdHash":"sha256:572439d01edf"} time="2026-06-26T17:35:22.488+00:00" traceId="[redacted trace id]"
```

Dependency contract checked: `node_modules/@anthropic-ai/sdk/src/resources/messages/messages.ts` defines `ToolResultBlockParam` with `content?: string | Array<...>` and `is_error?: boolean`; the observed provider response proves empty content is invalid when `is_error=true`.

Validation run after the patch:

```shell
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts
...
✓ |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts > Anthropic provider > replaces empty string error tool results with a non-empty payload
✓ |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts > Anthropic provider > replaces whitespace-only error tool results with a non-empty payload
✓ |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts > Anthropic provider > replaces invalid-surrogate-only error tool results with a non-empty payload
✓ |unit| src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts > Anthropic provider > drops empty text blocks from image tool results before Anthropic payloads

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  40 passed (40)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 209.51s

$ pnpm tsgo:test:src
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
# exited 0

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 192ms on 2 files using 16 threads.

$ git diff --check
# exited 0

$ .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.86)
```

### Crabbox evidence attempt

Requested Azure Crabbox proof could not acquire a lease in this shell because provider authentication is unavailable. No Crabbox lease id was created, so there is no remote workdir or remote test output to cite.

```shell
$ crabbox doctor --provider azure
failed  azure    DefaultAzureCredential: failed to acquire a token.
AzureCLICredential: ERROR: User [redacted] does not exist in MSAL token cache. Run `az login`.

$ az account get-access-token --resource https://management.azure.com/ --output json
ERROR: User [redacted] does not exist in MSAL token cache. Run `az login`.

$ crabbox doctor --provider aws
failed  aws      operation error EC2: DescribeInstances, get identity: get credentials: failed to refresh cached credentials, no EC2 IMDS role found

$ blacksmith testbox list --all
not authenticated -- run 'blacksmith auth login' first
```

Crabbox proof gap: the focused Anthropic regression test was not rerun on a remote Crabbox lease because all available remote backends require interactive/cloud authentication from this shell. The after-fix provider-boundary evidence remains the local targeted test/type/format/autoreview proof above plus the passing GitHub PR checks, including `check-test-types` and `build-artifacts`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Anthropic-compatible GitHub Copilot Claude requests no longer include `is_error=true` tool results with empty content after provider-boundary conversion.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source worktree on Node v26.3.0 and pnpm 11.2.2, using the real legacy Anthropic payload builder seam with mocked network dispatch stopped after payload capture.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm tsgo:test:src`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.

Evidence after fix: The terminal capture above shows the new regression cases passing, including empty string, whitespace-only, and invalid-surrogate-only error tool results being converted to non-empty content before outbound dispatch. The same capture shows the full targeted Anthropic provider test file passing with 40 tests.

Observed result after fix: The captured outbound Anthropic payload contains `content: "[tool error with no output]"` with `is_error: true` for empty error tool results, so it no longer matches the provider-rejected empty-content shape from the logs.

What was not tested: I did not run a live GitHub Copilot Claude request because that requires private Copilot credentials and replaying the affected private session state. The provider request-build/schema boundary that produced the logged 400 was covered locally.

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Seven coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

### 6. Restore unconfigured connection-error retry at the orchestrator layer (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts`)
Disabling SDK retries inside the prompt-lock release window (#3) is required so an in-window retry cannot widen the takeover race — but for users who have **not** configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries`, it also dropped the SDK's default retry of bare connection errors (`ECONNRESET`, `socket hang up`, `Connection error.`) that carry no leading HTTP status. This is restored at the orchestrator layer, where the existing one-shot `consumeTransientHttpRetry()` re-runs the full primary→fallback cycle and **re-acquires the session lock per attempt**, so it does not widen any single release window. The transient-retry gate now fires on `(isTransientHttp || isConnectionError)`; the new `isConnectionError` predicate reuses the canonical `INTERRUPTED_NETWORK_ERROR_RE` so it stays in sync with failover routing. In-window `maxRetries:0` is unchanged. This keeps the takeover-race fix intact while preserving unconfigured-user connection resilience.

### 7. Restore unconfigured request-timeout retry at the orchestrator layer (`agent-runner-execution.ts`)
The orchestrator transient-retry restore in #6 left a symmetric gap: in addition to bare connection errors, the OpenAI/Anthropic SDKs retry request-**timeout** transport errors by default, and after the in-window `maxRetries:0`, an unconfigured single-model user's timeout error is rethrown to the same outer gate — which only matched HTTP-status transients and connection errors, dropping timeout retry entirely (multi-model users were unaffected because failover already routes timeouts). The transient-retry gate now also fires on a new `isTransientTimeout` predicate (`isTimeoutErrorMessage`, the existing canonical timeout classifier), with its own `Transient timeout error` diagnostic label so the cause stays distinguishable in logs. The timeout disjunct is deliberately scoped to exclude non-transport timeout-looking messages: (a) timeout-only fallback summaries (`!isFallbackSummaryError` — multi-model failover already ran, so a redundant retry is wrong; this guard is scoped to the timeout disjunct only and does not change the connection path), and (b) CLI subprocess budget kills and Codex app-server bridge failures (`!hasDedicatedNonTransportTimeoutCopy` — these are subprocess kills / bridge failures, not transport timeouts, and have their own surfaced copy and replay handling). The one-shot `consumeTransientHttpRetry()` budget is shared, so timeout retry is bounded exactly like the connection retry. The connection-retry path semantics from #6 are unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

> **Refreshed at current head `e0ec982bb8` (proof captured at content-identical `0dc5a3010a`) for the connection-error retry-restoration change (Fix #6). Supersedes the earlier `bf4c277c9b` block below.**

- **Behavior addressed**: For users who have not configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries`, the in-window `maxRetries: 0` (required so an in-window SDK retry cannot widen the takeover race) previously also dropped the SDK's default retry of bare connection errors (ECONNRESET, socket hang up, `Connection error.`). That resilience is restored at the orchestrator layer, where each retry re-acquires the session lock and does not widen any single release window. The in-window `maxRetries: 0` race fix is unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0dc5a3010a`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, deps installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
- **Exact steps run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node_modules/.bin/vitest run <suite>`: the orchestrator connection-error retry cases (one-retry-then-success and bounded-loop), the HTTP transient 521 diagnostic case (the previously-red shard, now green with the preserved wording), the `isConnectionError` predicate unit coverage, the organic real-setup session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence), and the in-window session-lock suite; plus `tsgo:core`/`tsgo:core:test` typecheck and oxlint on the changed files.
- **Observed result**: connection errors now trigger exactly one full-cycle orchestrator retry (lock re-acquired per attempt) then succeed or terminate without looping; the HTTP diagnostic wording is preserved; the organic takeover still surfaces cause-neutral resend guidance; in-window `maxRetries: 0` is intact.
- **Evidence after fix** (current-head terminal output):
```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (current head 0dc5a3010a) ===
Environment: Darwin arm64, Node v24.10.0
Commit: 0dc5a3010af99cbbea1e0ea81851c6a756048bf2

--- A: Orchestrator-layer connection-error retry restoration (named cases) ---

--- C: isConnectionError predicate direct coverage ---
 ✓  agents-support  ../../src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts > isConnectionError > does not treat "invalid_api_key: the provided credential is incorrect" as a connection error 0ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  28 passed | 10 skipped (38)
   Duration  405ms (transform 183ms, setup 124ms, import 137ms, tests 33ms, environment 0ms)

--- D: Organic real-setup session-takeover proof (resend guidance, cause-neutral) ---

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
   Duration  1.25s (transform 494ms, setup 61ms, import 790ms, tests 340ms, environment 0ms)

--- E: In-window maxRetries:0 race fix unchanged (session-lock suite) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  242 passed (242)
   Duration  8.55s (transform 754ms, setup 126ms, import 1.63s, tests 6.67s, environment 0ms)

--- F: Code-path verification at current head ---
Orchestrator gate (connection OR http):
3282:      if ((isTransientHttp || isTransientConnection) && consumeTransientHttpRetry()) {
isConnectionError reuses canonical transport regex:
601:  if (isTimeoutErrorMessage(raw) || INTERRUPTED_NETWORK_ERROR_RE.test(raw)) {
751:  return INTERRUPTED_NETWORK_ERROR_RE.test(raw.trim());
In-window retry default still pins to 0:
2144:  const nextMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 0dc5a3010a ===
```
- **What was not tested**: The exact live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET production race is not replayed end-to-end; the strongest proof remains the deterministic local HTTP + on-disk session takeover coverage plus the orchestrator connection-retry regression above.

### Earlier proof (head `bf4c277c9b`)

> Supersedes the v6 proof below, which targeted older head `2fc3ecee2d`.

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <suite>` — the organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence), the retry-default live-ref unit suite (V1–V5), and the reply-runtime regression — plus `tsgo:test:src` for typecheck.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output captured at head `bf4c277c9b`:
```
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 ✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
      Tests  205 passed (205)
 tsgo:test:src exits 0 with no errors
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces explicit resend guidance ("⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.") instead of silently dropping the reply; the retry-default wrapper reads the shared live ref at call time so a repeat install refreshes the next call (including downward refresh when the configured value drops); and typecheck is clean at this head.
- **What was not tested**: The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


### Current-head proof (`bf4c277c9b`)

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `bf4c277c9b`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, freshly installed deps (`pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`).
- **Why refreshed**: The previous proof targeted an older head; the retry-default wrapper was reworked to read a shared live ref at call time (commit on this head), so proof is regenerated on the resulting head.

### 1. Organic session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence)
```
     ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  724ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```
The takeover error is thrown organically by the real prompt-lock fence (a concurrent steering write rewrites the same session file mid-call), and `runAgentTurnWithFallback` surfaces resend guidance instead of dropping the reply.

### 2. Retry-default live-ref unit proof (the wrapper fix, V1–V5)
```
 ✓  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (72 tests) 141ms
      Tests  72 passed (72)
```
Covers: refresh through the buried wrapper after lock-release wraps it; call-time live read so a repeat install changes the next call; downward refresh when the configured value drops (the original bug's essence); shared ref identity across both install orders; and no wrapper stacking on repeated installs.

### 3. Reply-runtime regression (no behavior regression on the takeover catch path)


### What was not tested
The live Feishu/DeepSeek ECONNRESET timing race that originally surfaced #87180 is not reproduced here; the takeover is reproduced deterministically via a real on-disk fence-drift, which exercises the same `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` path. The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.


<details>
<summary>Earlier proof (v6, head 2fc3ecee2d) — historical</summary>


- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Silent message loss on session takeover during the released prompt-lock window. When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally, an incoming steering write changed the transcript without updating the fence, raising `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, which `model-fallback.ts` aborted into a generic failure branch so the user saw no actionable output. After this fix the pure-takeover path returns explicit resend guidance, the SDK retry default is scoped to the embedded prompt-lock window (with configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` taking precedence), and `maxRetries` is forwarded through the native OpenAI transport.
- **Real environment tested**: Clean Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running the patched checkout at the current head commit `2fc3ecee2d`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built the patched checkout in the container and ran the affected suites plus a real-setup organic takeover test (real on-disk session file + real local `node:http` model server on an `openai-completions` baseUrl + real transport + real fence) and code-path verification, as captured in the v6 proof log below.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output from the patched container at head `2fc3ecee2d` (full v6 log reproduced in the section below):

```
$ docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d
--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact, parent 86150a3e51):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces a specific, actionable resend message (the after-fix payload line above) instead of the generic non-actionable failure seen at the parent; the configured provider retry survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options; the native transport forwards `maxRetries`; and the retry-default installer is idempotent with caller > configured > 0 precedence.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level `ECONNRESET` was run. The takeover proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (organic error), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

### Current-head proof (commit `2fc3ecee2d`, Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

Refreshed at the latest head, which adds the configured-provider-retry precedence. Run in a clean Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64):

```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d

--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ auto-reply  agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts (1 test)
   ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)

--- B: Embedded prompt retry default — idempotence + configured-override precedence ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

--- C: Configured provider retry survives through sdk.ts to native SDK options ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

--- D: Transport forwards maxRetries (native OpenAI) + failover classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  83 passed (83)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  241 passed (241)

--- E: Code-path verification at current head ---
installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault accepts configured default:
1088:  options?: { maxRetries?: number },
1098:  const defaultMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
Install site passes configured provider retry:
3994:              maxRetries: settingsManager.getProviderRetrySettings().maxRetries,

=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```

This confirms at the current head: the organic session-takeover path still yields visible resend guidance (A); the retry-default installer is idempotent and applies the configured-override precedence caller > configured > 0 (B); a configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options (C, the new regression); and the native transport forwards `maxRetries` (D).

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
AFTER fix (HEAD) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 487ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,
2380:          options?.maxRetries,
2614:          buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1083:    return innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...requestOptions });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.


</details>

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts` (real-setup takeover proof) | 1 |

New test coverage includes:
- Real-setup organic session-takeover proof: real session file + real HTTP model server + real transport + real fence producing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, asserting the after-fix resend guidance (and its absence at the parent)
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined
- `maxRetries`-only call returns an object (not `undefined`)
- Loopback end-to-end test: mock 500 server + `maxRetries: 0` → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)
- `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` default behavior (no caller options → maxRetries:0)
- Explicit `maxRetries` override preserved over embedded default
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- User-facing resend message for pure session takeover
- Preserved prompt error unwrapping from cleanup takeover
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter
- Request-timeout transport error triggers exactly one orchestrator retry then succeeds, and a persistent timeout is bounded to a single retry (no infinite loop)
- CLI subprocess timeouts and Codex app-server bridge failures are NOT retried through the transient gate and still surface their dedicated copy

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: fix(media): allow private openai compatible audio transcription endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Audio media-understanding transcription could target self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints, but audio config did not expose the private-network request opt-in used by guarded provider HTTP.
- Why it matters: Trusted local, RFC1918, or tailnet Whisper-style endpoints need explicit private-network access without globally relaxing media request SSRF policy.
- What changed: Added audio-only request config support for `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, aligned schema/generated metadata/docs, threaded audio request overrides into provider execution, and aligned OpenAI-compatible loopback audio auth with the chat-completion local auth pattern by using the shared `custom-local` marker only for loopback OpenAI-compatible audio bases.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Image and video media request config still reject `allowPrivateNetwork`, private-network access remains disabled by default, explicit `request.auth` still injects the configured auth header, and media/audio does not add the Ollama-specific local-auth branch.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Audio media config reused the generic media request schema/type path, which rejected `allowPrivateNetwork`, while provider execution needed an audio-specific trusted request override path. The local no-auth path also needed to follow the existing chat-completion pattern: synthesize the shared `custom-local` marker for loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoints, then avoid sending provider-default auth for that marker.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing schema and runner tests did not cover audio-specific private-network request overrides or loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints without provider auth.
- Contributing context (if known): The config docs baseline and audio field metadata are generated/aligned surfaces for this config contract.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Audio config accepts and forwards `allowPrivateNetwork`, image/video reject it, per-model audio request config works, loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can synthesize `custom-local` when provider auth is absent, synthetic local audio auth sends no provider-default `Authorization`, explicit request auth still wins, and Ollama does not get the media/audio local-auth branch.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests exercise the config contract and provider request assembly without needing a live transcription service.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can opt audio transcription requests into private-network endpoints with `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork` and per-model audio request config. Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints such as `http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1` can run without provider auth, matching local OpenAI-compatible chat-completion providers. The OpenAI provider docs now show the loopback example base URL.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The new network capability is an explicit audio-only opt-in for trusted private endpoints; defaults remain blocked by the SSRF guard and image/video still reject the private-network flag. Local no-auth audio is restricted to loopback OpenAI-compatible audio providers using the existing non-secret `custom-local` marker. Explicit request auth still injects the configured header when present.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.1.0-44-cloud-amd64 x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.0

### Steps

1. Parse audio, image, and video media config shapes through the runtime zod schema.
2. Run audio provider execution with provider-level, audio-level, and per-model request overrides.
3. Run provider HTTP request assembly with synthetic local auth and explicit request auth.

### Expected

- Audio request config accepts and forwards private-network opt-in only when explicitly configured.
- Image/video reject `allowPrivateNetwork` under media request config.
- Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can use the shared synthetic local auth marker when provider auth is absent.
- Synthetic local auth does not become a provider-default `Authorization` header.
- Explicit request auth overrides synthetic local auth and injects the configured header.
- Ollama does not receive a media/audio local-auth special case.

### Actual

- Matches expected in local tests and changed-gate validation.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

Set up a (WhisperLive)[https://github.com/collabora/WhisperLive] server locally. Modified openclaw.json to point to it with `media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork = true`:

```
"media": {
      "audio": {
        "enabled": true,
        "language": "en",
        "models": [
          {
            "provider": "openai",
            "model": "whisper-1",
            "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1",
          }
        ],
        "request": {
          "allowPrivateNetwork": true,
        }
      }
```

Verified audio transcription:

```
$ openclaw capability audio transcribe --file tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav --language en --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "audio.transcribe",
  "transport": "local",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "path": "/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav",
      "text": "Open Claw Media Transcription Test. The passphrase is Brass Lobster 7.",
      "kind": "audio.transcription"
    }

}
```



## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No PR review conversations currently exist.

- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes, optional new audio request config only
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Users could point audio transcription at private endpoints they do not fully trust.
  - Mitigation: The private-network exception is explicit, audio-only, disabled by default, documented as trusted-endpoint-only, and covered by schema/runtime tests.
- Risk: Synthetic local auth could accidentally be sent as provider-default auth.
  - Mitigation: OpenAI-compatible audio now uses the shared chat-completion local auth marker and omits provider-default auth for that marker while preserving explicit request auth overrides.

target.title: feat(tasks): improve task state transitions and lifecycle formatting
target.body:
## Summary
- add explicit intermediate active task states for approval waits and external waits
- propagate those active-state semantics across registry maintenance, flow guards, CLI views, and auto-reply task rendering
- add compressed operational summaries plus shared task lifecycle event formatting for state-change updates
- reuse the shared lifecycle formatter in task update delivery paths and extend focused task coverage

## Why
Previously, tasks moved through coarse active states like `queued` and `running`, which made it hard to distinguish between:
- work that is actively executing
- work that is alive but blocked on user approval
- work that is alive but waiting on an external dependency

This keeps the task model incremental while making the lifecycle more explicit and observable across the main task consumers.

## Notes
- intermediate task states are still projected as running at the `TaskFlow` layer for compatibility
- this PR also adds compressed operational summaries and lifecycle-oriented state-change formatting to make task progress easier to inspect across surfaces

## Testing
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.store.test.ts

target.title: fix: allow custom control UI origins
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` can include a custom desktop-app origin like `tauri://localhost`, but the gateway normalises that browser `Origin` header to the opaque `null` origin and never matches the explicit allowlist entry.
- Why it matters: Tauri/Electron-style Control UI wrappers have to fall back to wildcard origins even when operators want a specific origin allowlist.
- What changed: preserve the exact `scheme://host` origin for non-special URL schemes when the browser sends a scheme and host, then continue matching through the existing lower-case allowlist path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): literal `Origin: null` remains rejected, unlisted custom origins remain rejected, and host-header/local-loopback fallbacks keep their existing rules.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #46520
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `new URL("tauri://localhost").origin` returns the opaque string `null`, so exact allowlist matching compares `tauri://localhost` with `null`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the origin-check unit table did not include custom-scheme browser origins.
- Contributing context (if known): browser desktop wrappers can send non-special scheme origins even though those schemes do not have a standard web origin serialisation.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/origin-check.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: allowlisted custom-scheme origins match exactly after normalisation, while unlisted custom-scheme origins still fail.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is isolated to origin normalisation before the existing allowlist check.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Operators can set `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` to values like `tauri://localhost` without using `*` for desktop Control UI wrappers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
tauri://localhost -> URL.origin "null" -> allowlist miss

After:
tauri://localhost -> preserved custom origin -> explicit allowlist match
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A. This keeps explicit allowlist matching required for non-wildcard custom-scheme origins.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway Control UI origin check
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins = ["tauri://localhost"]`

### Steps

1. Configure `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` with `tauri://localhost`.
2. Connect with browser `Origin: tauri://localhost`.
3. Compare against `checkBrowserOrigin` behaviour.

### Expected

- The explicit custom-scheme allowlist entry is accepted.
- Unlisted custom-scheme origins are rejected.

### Actual

- Current main sees `new URL("tauri://localhost").origin` as `null`, so the explicit allowlist entry does not match.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local command evidence:

- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed.
- Manual Node parser check passed for `tauri://LOCALHOST`, `electron://localhost`, `https://CONTROL.example.com`, literal `null`, and malformed `not a url` cases.
- `pnpm docs:list` was attempted but blocked by Corepack registry timeout while downloading `pnpm 10.33.0` (`UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT`).
- `pnpm test src/gateway/origin-check.test.ts` reached the repo test wrapper via cached pnpm but could not run because this worktree has no `vitest` dependency and dependency installation is blocked by registry timeouts.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: source-level root cause in Node URL behaviour; exact custom-scheme origin preservation; literal `null` and malformed origins still return no parsed origin.
- Edge cases checked: upper-case custom origin host normalises to lower case; unlisted custom scheme remains rejected by the unit table.
- What you did **not** verify: local Vitest/full changed gate, because this machine cannot install missing npm dependencies from the registry. CI should run the new test.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: custom desktop wrapper origins could be over-accepted.
  - Mitigation: this only changes the parsed origin string; explicit allowlist, wildcard, host-header fallback, and local-loopback checks remain the same, and tests cover an unlisted custom-scheme rejection.

target.title: fix: recover stranded replies in message_tool_only mode (#85714)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.visibleReplies` is set to `message_tool`, the agent is expected to call the message tool to deliver replies. If the LLM forgets (a probabilistic event — the reporter saw it happen on the 8th turn after 7 correct deliveries), the reply was **silently discarded**: the substantive text sat in the JSONL transcript but was never delivered to the user.

The existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal()` detection was already identifying these stranded replies and logging a warning, but took no recovery action.

## Fix

This commit promotes the detection from log-only to **followup retry**, preserving the `message_tool_only` privacy contract:

1. **Gate room events**: Skip stranded reply detection for ambient `room_event` turns where `message_tool_only` is automatically set by the system. Silence on room events is intentional by design — the agent chose not to respond.

2. **Enqueue followup retry turn**: When a stranded reply is detected, enqueue a followup run whose prompt instructs the agent to deliver the captured text via `message(action=send)`. The agent itself calls the message tool, so delivery happens through the correct contract path — no suppression bypass, no privacy boundary crossing.

3. **Clear transcript/persistence fields**: The retry run explicitly clears `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` from the original followup run to prevent the original user prompt from hiding the retry instruction. The followup runner resolves `transcriptPrompt ?? extracted.text`, so a retained `transcriptPrompt` would cause the agent to see the original user question instead of the retry delivery instruction.

4. **No suppression bypass**: This does NOT mark payloads with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` or use `skipSuppressionCheck`. The `message_tool_only` privacy contract is fully preserved: delivery only happens when the agent explicitly calls `message(action=send)`.

Normal (non-stranded) paths are unaffected: short replies, intentional `NO_REPLY`, and runs where the message tool already delivered are all excluded by the existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal` guard.

### Why followup retry instead of bypass delivery?

The `message_tool_only` contract says: "no message tool call = private/silent". Auto-publishing stranded text would break this documented behavior. Instead, we give the agent a second chance to follow the contract correctly — like returning an undelivered letter and asking the sender to mail it properly, rather than opening and publishing it ourselves.

## Changed files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` — followup retry with cleaned transcript fields, room_event gate (+26/-3 net)
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` — 5 new retry/gate tests replacing 6 bypass tests (-4 net)

## Tests

| Suite | Count | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| `agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` | 47 | ✅ All pass |
| `private-message-tool-final.test.ts` | 8 | ✅ All pass |
| `dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | 189 | ✅ All pass (unchanged, confirms no regressions) |
| `followup-runner.test.ts` | 67 | ✅ All pass (confirms followup queue compatibility) |

New test cases:
- `enqueues a followup retry for a stranded substantive reply (#85714)` — verifies `enqueueFollowupRun` is called with retry prompt containing the assistant text
- `does not enqueue retry for short private final replies` — negative case
- `does not enqueue retry when the message tool delivered this turn` — negative case
- `does not enqueue retry for room_event turns (#85714)` — verifies room_event gate
- `clears transcriptPrompt on retry to prevent original prompt from hiding retry instruction (#85714)` — regression test verifying `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` are all `undefined` on the retry run

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent's final message silently discarded when LLM forgets to call the message tool in `message_tool_only` mode. The fix enqueues a followup retry turn so the agent can deliver via `message(action=send)`, preserving the privacy contract.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0, patched checkout at `9dc3c76dc3`.
- **Privacy contract preserved**: The fix does NOT use `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` or `skipSuppressionCheck`. No payload is auto-delivered. The agent is re-prompted to call `message(action=send)` itself.
- **Transcript isolation**: The retry run clears `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` so the followup runner's `transcriptPrompt ?? extracted.text` resolution correctly uses the retry prompt, not the original user message.
- **Room event safety**: Stranded reply detection is gated by `sessionCtx.InboundEventKind !== "room_event"`, so ambient room events (where silence is intentional) never trigger recovery. Covered by dedicated test case.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, `warnPrivateMessageToolFinal` only logs — no retry is enqueued and the reply is silently lost.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  ✓ auto-reply agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts (47 tests) 1729ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  47 passed (47)

  ✓ unit-fast private-message-tool-final.test.ts (8 tests) 2ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  8 passed (8)

  ✓ auto-reply dispatch-from-config.test.ts (189 tests) 3770ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  189 passed (189)

  ✓ auto-reply followup-runner.test.ts (67 tests) 1710ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  67 passed (67)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: When a stranded reply is detected, `enqueueFollowupRun` is called with a retry prompt containing the original assistant text and instructions to call `message(action=send)`. The retry run has `transcriptPrompt: undefined` so the followup runner correctly uses the retry instruction. Short replies, already-delivered replies, room events, and runs with existing transcript prompts are all verified by dedicated test cases.
- **What was not tested**: Live end-to-end delivery through an actual channel — the test verifies that the correct followup run is enqueued with the expected prompt shape and cleaned fields. The followup queue machinery (`enqueueFollowupRun` → `scheduleFollowupDrain` → `runReplyAgent`) is a well-tested existing mechanism used for concurrent message queuing.

## Real behavior proof (end-to-end, qa-lab host runner)

This adds a deterministic end-to-end proof that drives the real runtime loop — `runReplyAgent → stranded-final detection → warnPrivateMessageToolFinal → enqueueFollowupRun("stranded-reply-retry") → followup-runner → runEmbeddedAgent → message(action=send) → qa-channel outbound` — not the unit mocks (which stub `enqueueFollowupRun`/`runEmbeddedAgent`).

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Under `messages.visibleReplies=message_tool` (resolved `message_tool_only`), a substantive final reply that never calls the message tool was kept private and silently lost. The fix detects the stranded final and enqueues exactly one bounded followup retry that re-prompts the agent to deliver via `message(action=send)`, preserving the privacy contract; if the retry strands again, no second retry is enqueued.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 24.3.0 arm64, Node.js v24.10.0, branch `fix/stranded-reply-pending-delivery-85714` at current HEAD `0469009210` (rebased on latest upstream/main; recovery default-off + collect-batching isolation + updated scenario). qa-lab host runner: in-process gateway child + qa-channel bus + scenario-aware `mock-openai` provider (deterministic by construction; no live model, no Docker).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran the new scenario, PASS:
  ```
  node scripts/run-node.mjs qa suite \
    --provider-mode mock-openai \
    --scenario message-tool-stranded-final-recovery \
    --concurrency 1 --fast
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: captured scenario result and the gateway's own runtime log line (ids redacted):
  ```
  COUNTS: { total: 1, passed: 1, failed: 0 }
  step: pass - recovered=1; stranded turns=1; retry delivery turns=1; WARN logged=true

  2026-06-06T06:31:23.411+08:00 [source-reply/private-final] agent produced a long private final reply without calling the configured delivery tool (message_tool_only); response kept private and not delivered to the source channel
  ```
  Asserted from the live request/outbound capture:
  - Turn 1 request: `plannedToolName !== "message"` (long plain-text final, stranded).
  - Exactly one retry request plans the message tool, detected via the verbatim retry needle `you did not call message(action=send)`.
  - Retry delivery request: `plannedToolName === "message"`, `plannedToolArgs.action === "send"`, `plannedToolArgs.message === "QA-STRANDED-85714"`; one outbound on the direct conversation carried the marker (`recovered=1`).
  - After an 8s settle: outbound with the marker stays exactly 1 and message-tool retry deliveries stay exactly 1 (no loop).
- **Observed result after fix**: the stranded `message_tool_only` final was detected and warned, exactly one retry was enqueued, the retry delivered the original text via `message(action=send)` to the channel, and no second retry/delivery occurred — all four invariants observed against the real delivery path.
- **What was not tested**: not run against a live frontier model (deterministic mock by design, since a real model "forgetting" the tool is non-deterministic), and not on a real chat transport (Telegram/WhatsApp/Discord) — exercised on the in-process qa-channel bus; the `message(action=send)` → source-conversation outbound contract is transport-agnostic core auto-reply behavior.

Proof scaffolding added (business logic under `src/auto-reply/` untouched): `qa/scenarios/channels/message-tool-stranded-final-recovery.md` (new scenario) and `extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.ts` (two additive deterministic decision branches following the existing `qa group visible reply tool check` pattern).

Closes #85714

target.title: fix: page sessions_history beyond truncated tails
target.body:
Related: #90981

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where parent agents using `sessions_history` could only inspect the newest bounded tail of long child sessions. When that tail was truncated or started inside late-session context, there was no supported way to page backward through older transcript windows without reading raw JSONL files.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds explicit `offset` pagination to `sessions_history` and Gateway `chat.history`, returning `offset`, `nextOffset`, `hasMore`, and `totalMessages` metadata for explicit offset requests. The implementation keeps the existing no-offset newest-tail behavior, keeps display sanitization and byte caps, computes continuation from the final returned page boundary, overreads one context row for stale announce-pair filtering, and keeps offset pages scoped to OpenClaw transcript windows instead of merging whole external CLI fallback imports into every page.

This does not add a readable full-history export surface; #90981 should remain open for that broader product/API decision if maintainers still want it.

## User Impact

Agents and operators can now call `sessions_history` with `offset: 0`, then follow `nextOffset` to page backward through long child-session transcripts. This makes recovery and audit workflows deterministic while preserving the bounded, redacted history view.

## Evidence

Before evidence:

```text
2026-06-26T02:36:12.812Z CALL sessions_history limit=3 for multiple child sessions
2026-06-26T02:36:17.234Z CALL sessions_history limit=2 for the affected child session
2026-06-26T02:36:19.990Z CALL sessions_history limit=1 for the affected child session
2026-06-26T02:36:23.101Z CALL sessions_history includeTools=false limit=5 for the affected child session

Observed result: calls stayed anchored to newest tail slices and exposed no cursor/offset to retrieve older omitted windows.
```

Behavior addressed:

`sessions_history` and Gateway `chat.history` now support explicit offset pagination for bounded, sanitized OpenClaw transcript windows.

Real environment tested:

Local OpenClaw source checkout in a Codex worktree on Linux/WSL2, Node/pnpm dependencies already installed. Real live recovery logs from June 26, 2026 were used as before-fix evidence; no live provider replay was run after the patch because the bug is in local transcript pagination/schema handling before model generation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/session-tool.md docs/concepts/delegate-architecture.md docs/tools/subagents.md
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.packages.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-packages.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 51.59s
Docs formatting clean (669 files).
tsgo core: passed
tsgo test src: passed
tsgo test packages: passed
git diff --check: passed
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.78)
```

Observed result after fix:

Focused tests cover `offset` validation and forwarding, first-page `nextOffset`, nonzero page reads, stale announce-pair boundary overread, final byte-budget cursor calculation, embedded gateway parity, protocol schema acceptance, and docs/tool metadata updates. The final autoreview found no accepted/actionable findings.

Follow-up evidence for the P1 review finding:

```text
commit 9800182da19f52c118671608f84f0ba19546b34b recomputes sessions_history pagination from the final tool-visible messages after includeTools filtering, sanitization, and the 80 KiB tool cap. It also preserves __openclaw.seq on the oversized-message placeholder so the cursor still advances accurately when the last surviving row is replaced.

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 65.91s

.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.87)
```


Azure Crabbox real-history proof for the P1 review finding:

target.title: docs(feishu): 使用子目录结构存放动态 agent workspace
target.body:
## 变更

将 Feishu 动态 agent 创建的示例配置和默认值从同级文件结构改为子目录结构：

| 配置项 | 旧值 | 新值 |
|--------|------|------|
| workspaceTemplate | ~/.openclaw/workspace-{agentId} | ~/.openclaw/workspaces/{agentId} |
| agentDirTemplate | ~/.openclaw/agents/{agentId}/agent | {agentId} |

## 原因

- 主目录整洁：所有动态 agent 收拢在 workspaces/ 子目录下，不会跟 openclaw.json、logs/、plugins/ 等混在一起
- 可扩展：以后加 agents/、backups/ 等目录结构清晰
- 批量操作方便：ls ~/.openclaw/workspaces/ 一眼看全，rm -rf workspaces/ 可整体清理
- 生产验证：该结构已在实际部署中运行（9+ 个 per-user workspace）

## 影响范围

仅修改文档中的示例配置和默认值表格，不影响现有代码行为。

target.title: fix(codex): refresh app inventory after upgrades
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Codex app inventory cache keys did not encode the loaded OpenClaw host and Codex plugin build identities, allowing cache scopes from different loaded builds to collide across runtime or plugin module lifecycle boundaries.

## Why This Change Was Made

The app inventory cache key now includes explicit OpenClaw and Codex plugin build versions alongside the existing runtime identity. The Codex wrapper supplies the host version and resolves the installed plugin package version across source and bundled layouts.

## User Impact

Codex app discovery uses a distinct inventory cache scope when a runtime or plugin module is loaded with a different OpenClaw or Codex plugin version. This does not add same-process package-update detection; version changes are observed when the corresponding runtime/module lifecycle loads the new build.

## Evidence

- Focused Codex cache tests: 2 files passed, 12 tests passed.
- Focused `oxfmt` and `oxlint`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- Autoreview: clean, with no accepted or actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app inventory cache keys must differ for runtime/plugin module instances loaded with different OpenClaw host or Codex plugin versions.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node.js 24.14.1, using the production `buildCodexAppInventoryCacheKey` implementation from an isolated `.mem/integ` OpenClaw workspace.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Executed the production TypeScript module through Node.js with otherwise-identical runtime inputs, changing only the OpenClaw host version and then only the Codex plugin version. The executable proof was verified with `uvx showboat verify .mem/integ/proofs/codex-app-inventory-version-key.md`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
{
  "baseline": {
    "openClawVersion": "2026.6.27",
    "codexPluginVersion": "2026.6.10",
    "codexHome": "/isolated/codex",
    "endpoint": null,
    "runtimeIdentity": null,
    "authProfileId": "proof",
    "accountId": null,
    "envApiKeyFingerprint": null,
    "appServerVersion": null
  },
  "hostVersionChangesKey": true,
  "pluginVersionChangesKey": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The serialized production key contains both explicit build versions. Changing only the loaded host version changed the key, and changing only the loaded plugin version changed the key. The Showboat proof replayed successfully; focused Vitest also passed 2 files and 12 tests.
- What was not tested: No same-process hot package update, live gateway, deployment, devbox, or external Codex app-server was started or modified. The patch does not claim that an already-evaluated module rereads changed package metadata without a reload.
